name: Bag

aliases.name: Bag
aliases.comment:
type alias of a bag.

Use the encode function to access elements in the dictionary.
aliases.type: { content : Dict.Dict comparable ( a, Basics.Int ), encode : a -> comparable }

values.name: count
values.comment:
Count the amounts of an element in the bag

    empty identity
        |> count "🍎"
        --> 0

.

    [ ( "🍎", 42 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> count "🍎"
        --> 42

.

    [ ( "🍊", 42 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> count "🍎"
        --> 0

values.name: empty
values.comment:
Construct an empty bag

    empty identity |> toAssociationList --> []

values.name: fromAssociationList
values.comment: Construct from an association list
values.type: (a -> comparable) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> Bag.Bag comparable a

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Counts the occurrences of elements in a bag.

    []
        |> fromList identity
        |> toAssociationList
        --> []

.

    [ "🍎", "🍎" ]
        |> fromList identity
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍎",2)]

.

    [ "🍇", "🍎", "🍊" ]
        |> fromList identity
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",1)]

.

    [ "🍇", "🍎", "🍊", "🍎", "🍊", "🍎" ]
        |> fromList identity
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",2),("🍎",3)]
values.type: (a -> comparable) -> List.List a -> Bag.Bag comparable a

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert an element into the bag

    empty identity
        |> insert 1 "🍎"
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍎",1)]

.

    [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍇", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> insert 2 "🍎"
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",3)]

.

    [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍇", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> insert 1 "🍎"
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",1)]
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a

values.name: intersection
values.comment:
Intersect two bags.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> intersection (empty identity)
        |> toAssociationList
        --> []

.

    empty identity
        |> intersection (
            [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
                |> fromAssociationList identity
        )
        |> toAssociationList
        --> []

.

    [ ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> intersection (
            [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ) ]
                |> fromAssociationList identity
        )
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍎",1)]

.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍊", 3 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> intersection (
            [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍇", 3 ) ]
                |> fromAssociationList identity
        )
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",2)]
values.type: Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment:
Check if the bag is empty

    empty identity
        |> isEmpty
        --> True

    singleton identity "🍎"
        |> isEmpty
        --> False

values.name: member
values.comment:
Compute if the element is a member

    member "🍎" (empty identity) --> False

.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> member "🍎"
        --> True

.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> member "🍒"
        --> False

values.name: minus
values.comment:
subtracts the first bag from the second.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> minus (empty identity)
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",1)]

.

    empty identity
        |> minus (
            [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
                |> fromAssociationList identity
        )
        |> toAssociationList
        --> []

.

    [ ( "🍇", 2 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍊", 2 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> minus (
            [ ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
                |> fromAssociationList identity
        )
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",2),("🍊",1)]
values.type: Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a

values.name: plus
values.comment:
Combine two bags.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> plus (
            [ ( "🍒", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ) ]
                |> fromAssociationList identity
        )
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",1),("🍒",1)]

.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> plus (singleton identity "🍎")
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",2)]

.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> plus (
            [ ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍒", 1 ) ]
                |> fromAssociationList identity
        )
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",2),("🍒",1)]
values.type: Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Remove an element from the bag

    empty identity
        |> remove 1 "🍎"
        |> toAssociationList
        --> []

.

    [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍇", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> remove 1 "🍎"
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1),("🍎",1)]

.

    [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍇", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> remove 1 "🍎"
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1)]

.

    [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍇", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> remove 1 "🍎"
        |> toAssociationList
        --> [("🍇",1),("🍊",1)]
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> Bag.Bag comparable a -> Bag.Bag comparable a

values.name: singleton
values.comment:
Construct a bag with a single element

    singleton identity "🍎" |> toAssociationList --> [ ( "🍎", 1 ) ]

values.name: size
values.comment:
Counts all elements in the bag

    size (empty identity) --> 0

.

    [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍊", 3 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> size
        --> 6

values.name: toAssociationList
values.comment: Convert into an association list
values.type: Bag.Bag comparable a -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int )

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert into a list with repeating elements

    empty identity
        |> toList
         --> []

.

    [ ( "🍎", 2 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> toList
        --> ["🍎","🍎"]

.

    [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍇", 1 ) ]
        |> fromAssociationList identity
        |> toList
        --> ["🍇","🍊","🍎","🍎"]

name: List.Bag

values.name: count
values.comment:
Count the amounts of an element in the list

    count "🍎" [] --> 0

    count "🍎" [ ( "🍎", 42 ) ] --> 42

    count "🍎" [ ( "🍊", 42 ) ] --> 0

values.name: empty
values.comment:
Construct an empty list

    empty --> []

values.name: insert
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int )

values.name: intersection
values.type: List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int )

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment:
Check if the list is empty

    isEmpty [] --> True

    isEmpty [ ( "🍎", 42 ) ] --> False

values.name: member
values.comment:
Compute if the element is a member

    member "🍎" [] --> False

    member "🍎" [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ] --> True

    member "🍒" [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 1 ), ( "🍊", 1 ) ] --> False

values.name: minus
values.type: List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int )

values.name: plus
values.type: List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int )

values.name: remove
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int ) -> List.List ( a, Basics.Int )

values.name: singleton
values.comment:
Construct a list with a single element

    singleton "🍎" --> [ ( "🍎", 1 ) ]

values.name: size
values.comment:
Counts all elements in the list

    size [] --> 0

    size [ ( "🍇", 1 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍊", 3 ) ] --> 6

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert into a list with repeating elements

    toList [] --> []

    toList [ ( "🍎", 2 ) ] --> ["🍎","🍎"]

    toList [ ( "🍊", 1 ), ( "🍎", 2 ), ( "🍇", 1 ) ] --> ["🍊","🍎","🍎","🍇"]
